text: Clean code is essential for any programmer who wants to work efficiently with others or on their own future projects. The most important quality of clean code is that it is readable. To achieve this, you should follow clear naming conventions — variables and functions should have names that describe their purpose well, like `calculateTotalPrice` instead of `ctp`. Furthermore, break your code into small, focused functions that do just one thing. This makes the overall program much easier to understand and test. Ultimately, writing clean code is an investment in maintainability; it allows you and your team to modify and extend the codebase with confidence over time.
